data.title: Equity Bancshares stock slides on pricing capital raise of about $80M via stock offering
data.description:
• Equity Bancshares (NYSE:EQBK) announces the pricing of its underwritten public offering of 1.80M shares of its Class A common stock at $44.50 per share to raise $80 million.

  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 269,640 shares of common stock at the public offering price.

  • The company intends to use the proceeds of the offering to support its continued growth, the potential repayment of existing subordinated debt, and other general corporate purposes.

  • The company intends to close the offering on or about December 4, 2024.

  • EQBK shares were trading -1.46% after-hours on Monday.

data.title: No confidence: French government likely to collapse over budget impasse
data.description:
The French government is expected to collapse this week as far-right party National Rally and a left-wing coalition submitted no-confidence motions against Prime Minister Michel Barnier, with the vote likely to be held on Wednesday.

This follows months of political uncertainty in France, starting when President Emmanuel Macron called for a snap election in June, which resulted in a hung parliament.

The main point of contention is the annual social security budget, which needs to be approved this month, and the spiraling deficit. The budget bill presented by Barnier's government included €60B ($63.17B) of tax hikes and spending cuts, which was welcomed by the European Commission.

But Barnier failed to secure enough support in France for the bill, and chose a constitutional provision to force the legislation's adoption without a vote, risking a no-confidence vote.

"While a last-minute twist is still possible, all indications are that the government will have fallen by the end of the week," said Charlotte de Montpellier, senior economist, ING. "It's almost certain that there will not be a majority to pass a state budget or a social security budget before the end of the year."

A provisional budget, similar to the 2024 budget, may be implemented. But this would not fix the trajectory of public spending, and Paris will not be able to meet the commitments it made to European authorities.

France's benchmark index CAC 40 (CAC:IND) rose 0.6% early on Tuesday. It ended flat on Monday in choppy trade after dropping as much as 1.2% earlier in the day. To note, the index has declined over 9% since Macron called for a snap election.

More on France

  • France's Economic Outlook Darkens
  • EWQ: A Good Way To Access France When The Trend Turns
  • Political deadlock in France as leftists score shock victory
  • French stocks soar after National Rally notches big win

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: ZS, SMCI, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched up Tuesday morning as investors awaited the release of October's Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) later today.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Credo Technology (NASDAQ:CRDO) shares soared over 32% after delivering strong FQ2 results, with revenue climbing 64% Y/Y and an upbeat FQ3 outlook. The company anticipates FQ3 revenue of $115M-$125M, significantly above the $86.04M consensus, and adjusted gross margins of 61%-63%.
  • Sunstone Hotel Investors (NYSE:SHO) shares soared 9% following reports that Trinity Investments has expressed interest in acquiring the company, potentially offering about $13 per share. Sunstone, with a market cap of $2.17B, has seen its shares remain largely unchanged this year.
  • Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) climbed another 4% on Tuesday, building on its 28% surge in the prior session, after an independent review found no evidence of misconduct by the board or audit committee. The investigation followed concerns raised by former auditor Ernst & Young, but the findings contradicted those claims, though some procedural lapses were noted. BDO USA, its new auditor, submitted a compliance plan to Nasdaq to avoid delisting and implemented governance changes, including naming Kenneth Cheung as chief accounting officer and plans to add a new CFO, chief compliance officer, and general counsel. Super Micro remains confident it will meet its compliance timeline and confirmed no need to revise quarterly reports for FY2024 or prior years.

Biggest stock losers

  • Zscaler (NASDAQ:ZS) fell 8% despite posting FQ1 results that beat expectations, as its Q2 profit guidance slightly missed forecasts. The company projects Q2 revenue of $633M-$635M, with the midpoint above the $633.8M consensus, and adjusted EPS of $0.68-$0.69, just below the $0.70 estimate. For FY2025, Zscaler raised its revenue guidance to $2.62B-$2.64B, up from $2.6B-$2.62B, exceeding the $2.61B consensus. Additionally, CFO Remo Canessa announced his retirement after nearly eight years in the position.
  • TransMedics (NASDAQ:TMDX) shares plunged nearly 9% after narrowing its FY2024 revenue guidance to $428M-$432M from the previous $425M-$445M range, slightly below the $432.37M consensus. Despite the adjustment, the updated outlook represents robust growth of 77%-79% compared to 2023 revenue. Additionally, the company announced the appointment of Gerardo Hernandez as CFO, effective December 2, 2024, succeeding Stephen Gordon.

data.title: HEALWELL acquires 51% stake in Mutuo Health Solutions for $5.5M in cash and stock deal
data.description:
• HEALWELL (OTCQX:HWAIF) has acquired a 51% controlling interest in Mutuo Health Solutions, a Toronto-based medical artificial intelligence company focused on developing predictive clinical AI technology, for $5.5 million in cash and shares.
  • Mutuo's flagship product, AutoScribe, developed by Mutuo in 2018, is a Canadian healthcare platform that focuses on precision, security, and integration. Mutuo developed AutoScribe through collaboration with the University of Toronto and Unity Health Toronto. AutoScribe captures and organizes patient data in real time, enhancing clinical accuracy and operational efficiency in healthcare.
  • The move will expand its AI-driven healthcare solutions. The company plans to integrate AutoScribe into its clinical decision support co-pilots, providing advanced digital assistance to healthcare providers, to address the growing need for documentation and EMR automation in clinics and hospitals.
  • The acquisition strengthens HEALWELL's position as a leader in predictive AI solutions for improved patient care and clinical workflows.

data.title: Ampere, STMicroelectronics ink long-term silicon carbide supply agreement
data.description:
• Renault group's electrical unit Ampere, and STMicroelectronics (NYSE:STM) on Tuesday reported the next step in their strategic co-operation, starting in 2026, with a multi-year agreement on the supply of Silicon Carbide (SiC) power modules.

  • Ampere and STMicroelectronics worked together on the optimization of the power module, the key element in the powerbox.

  • “By working upstream together, we were able to optimize and secure the supply of key components for our electric powertrains, to offer high performance EVs with increased range and optimized charging time,” said Philippe Brunet, SVP Powertrain & EV engineering, Ampere.
  • It aligns perfectly with Ampere's strategy to master the entire value chain of power electronics for its e-powertrain, leveraging STMicroelectronics’ (STM) expertise in power modules, Brunet added.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hexagon Composites appoints Philipp Schramm as CEO
data.description:
• Hexagon Composites (OTCPK:HXGCF) announces the appointment of Philipp Schramm as CEO, effective 7 January 2025, to succeed Jon Erik Engeset, who on 16 April 2024 announced his decision to step down.
  • Philipp most recently served as the CEO and CFO of Brose, one of the world's largest family-owned automotive parts suppliers.

data.title: Blackstone prices $750M debt offering via issuance of senior notes due 2034
data.description:
• Blackstone (NYSE:BX) has priced its offering of $750 million of 5.000% senior notes due 2034 of Blackstone Reg Finance Co. L.L.C., its indirect subsidiary.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the notes offering for general corporate purposes.

data.title: NATO chief to Trump: Unfavorable Ukraine peace deal would be 'dire threat' to U.S.
data.description:
NATO's secretary general Mark Rutte has warned U.S. president-elect Donald Trump of a "dire security threat" from Russia, North Korea, China and Iran if Ukraine is forced to sign a peace deal that's more favorable for Moscow, according to an interview with the Financial Times.

"We cannot have a situation where we have Kim Jong Un, the Russian leader [Vladimir Putin], Xi Jinping and Iran high-fiving because we came to a deal which is not good for Ukraine… long term, that will be a dire security threat not only to Europe but also to the U.S.," Rutte told the FT in his first interview as head of the alliance.

Rutte said he spoke to Trump about the potential threat last month as part of an effort to ensure continued U.S. support for Ukraine. Rutte said he noted the missile technology being sent from Russia into North Korea, "which is posing a dire threat not only to South Korea, Japan, but also to the U.S. mainland."

"Iran is getting money from Russia in return for missiles, also drone technology. And the money is being used to prop up Hezbollah and Hamas, but also steering conflict beyond the region."

Rutte also pointed to Chinese President Xi "watching very carefully what comes out of this," referring to how Beijing may be emboldened to invade Taiwan if Russia seizes more territory in Ukraine.

As for Ukraine's repeated requests to join NATO, Rutte said boosting military support for Kyiv was more important. This would ensure "that whenever [Volodymyr] Zelenskyy decides to get into peace talks, he can do this from a position of strength."

Rutte's statements come as NATO leaders are set to meet on December 3-4 in Brussels. U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken will attend the meeting, where he will discuss further military support for Ukraine and the country's inclusion in the alliance.

More on Ukraine war

  • U.S. said to ready $725M arms package for Ukraine
  • Ukraine reportedly needs tens of thousands of uncrewed vehicles
  • Arms sales rise on demand linked to Ukraine and Gaza wars, Asia tensions

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Zscaler tops FQ1 estimates; initiates FQ2 and raised FY guidance
Corpay CFO Tom Panther to step down in 2025
data.description:
• Corpay (NYSE:CPAY) announced that its CFO, Tom Panther, is set to leave the company on March 15, 2025, to join the National Christian Foundation as their CFO.
  • The firm has begun the process of finding a new CFO to take his place.
  • Panther has pledged to support a smooth transition to the next CFO.
  • In addition to this executive change, it is currently expecting its fourth quarter 2024 organic revenue & earnings results to be in line with the guidance provided on November 7, 2024.
